methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Rivet Industries is a Seed-stage frontier-tech company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, supplemented by a broader universe of 10 public and private comps including Tenna Systems, Scale AI, and Stark Defence.

The peer-median method provides the primary triangulation anchor. The five direct comparables—spanning Seed to Series A stages—yield a median valuation of $300M, with valuations ranging from $60M (Tenna Systems at Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI at Series A). This wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity typical of early-stage defense tech, where outcomes and capital intensity vary materially. The median of $300M is less skewed by Scale AI's outlier position than the mean would be, making it a more robust central estimate for a Seed-stage company.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. This 15% bandwidth around the median reflects the internal consistency of the peer set but does not materially account for stage discount or Rivet's specific market position, given limited data on revenue traction, IP differentiation, or market timing. Key uncertainties include comparability of product-market fit across defense subsectors and the applicability of Series A valuations to a Seed-stage entry point. Further analysis would benefit from revenue multiples or customer concentration data.
